title: Cache penetration — non-existent IDs bypass cache and always hit DB
expected_feedback: Standard: When a non-existent ID is requested, the cache always misses and the DB always returns no rows — but this 'not found' result is never cached. An attacker or misbehaving client can enumerate random IDs to bypass the cache layer entirely and overload the database (cache penetration). Defense: cache the null/not-found result with a short TTL (30-60 seconds): rdb.Set(ctx, key, '__NULL__', 60*time.Second). For APIs with user-supplied IDs, also consider a bloom filter as a first-line defense.
